author: The Incarnation of the Word
book: On the Incarnation of the Word.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: On the Incarnation of the Word.

Argument (1) from the withdrawal of prophecy and destruction of Jerusalem, (2) from the conversion of the Gentiles, and that to the God of Moses. What more remains for the Messiah to do, that Christ has not done?

title: Argument (1) from the withdrawal of prophecy and destruction of Jerusalem, (2) from the conversion of the Gentiles, and that to the God of Moses. What more remains for the Messiah to do, that Christ has not done?

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Argument (1) from the withdrawal of prophecy and destruction of Jerusalem, (2) from the conversion of the Gentiles, and that to the God of Moses.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: What more remains for the Messiah to do, that Christ has not done?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: So the Jews are trifling, and the time in question, which they refer to the future, is actually come.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For when did prophet and vision cease from Israel, save when Christ came, the Holy of Holies?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For it is a sign, and an important proof, of the coming of the Word of God, that Jerusalem no longer stands, nor is any prophet raised up nor vision revealed to them,—and that very naturally.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For when He that was signified was come, what need was there any longer of any to signify Him?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: When the truth was there, what need any more of the shadow?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For this was the reason of their prophesying at all,—namely, till the true Righteousness should come, and He that was to ransom the sins of all.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And this was why Jerusalem stood till then—namely, that there they might be exercised in the types as a preparation for the reality.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: So when the Holy of Holies was come, naturally vision and prophecy were sealed and the kingdom of Jerusalem ceased.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For kings were to be anointed among them only until the Holy of Holies should have been anointed; and Jacob prophesies that the kingdom of the Jews should be established until Him, as follows:—“The ruler shall not fail from Juda, nor the Prince from his loins, until that which is laid up for him shall come; and he is the expectation of the nations.”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Whence the Saviour also Himself cried aloud and said: “The law and the prophets prophesied until John.”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: If then there is now among the Jews king or prophet or vision, they do well to deny the Christ that is come.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But if there is neither king nor vision, but from that time forth all prophecy is sealed and the city and temple taken, why are they so irreligious and so perverse as to see what has happened, and yet to deny Christ, Who has brought it all to pass?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or why, when they see even heathens deserting their idols, and placing their hope, through Christ, on the God of Israel, do they deny Christ, Who was born of the root of Jesse after the flesh and henceforth is King?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For if the nations were worshipping some other God, and not confessing the God of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob and Moses, then, once more, they would be doing well in alleging that God had not come.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But if the Gentiles are honouring the same God that gave the law to Moses and made the promise to Abraham, and Whose word the Jews dishonoured,—why are they ignorant, or rather why do they choose to ignore, that the Lord foretold by the Scriptures has shone forth upon the world, and appeared to it in bodily form, as the Scripture said: “The Lord God hath shined upon us;” and again: “He sent His Word and healed them;” and again: “Not a messenger, not an angel, but the Lord Himself saved them?”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Their state may be compared to that of one out of his right mind, who sees the earth illumined by the sun, but denies the sun that illumines it.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For what more is there for him whom they expect to do, when he is come?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: To call the heathen?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But they are called already.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: To make prophecy, and king, and vision to cease?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: This too has already come to pass.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: To expose the godlessness of idolatry?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: It is already exposed and condemned.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or to destroy death?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: He is already destroyed.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: What then has not come to pass, that the Christ must do?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: What is left unfulfilled, that the Jews should now disbelieve with impunity?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For if, I say,—which is just what we actually see,—there is no longer king nor prophet nor Jerusalem nor sacrifice nor vision among them, but even the whole earth is filled with the knowledge of God, and Gentiles, leaving their godlessness, are now taking refuge with the God of Abraham, through the Word, even our Lord Jesus Christ, then it must be plain, even to those who are exceedingly obstinate, that the Christ is come, and that He has illumined absolutely all with His light, and given them the true and divine teaching concerning His Father.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: So one can fairly refute the Jews by these and by other arguments from the Divine Scriptures.
